mnemonics.title: PUE Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember PUE = Power Used for Equipment. Think of it as 'PUE = Energy In / Energy Out to IT' - closer to 1.0 is better.

mnemonics.title: Data Center Cooling Phrase
mnemonics.content: Hot goes out, Cold goes in - Aisle contain, Efficiency to win! (Hot/cold aisle containment)

acronyms.usage: Metric to measure data center energy efficiency. Lower is better (ideal is 1.0)
Inverse of PUE as percentage. Higher is better (100% is ideal)
Computing equipment including servers, storage, and networking devices
Systems used for cooling and climate control in data centers
Specialized cooling units designed for data center environments
Backup power systems to ensure continuous operation during power outages

visualTips.visualization: Think of PUE as a ratio: Total Energy (building) / IT Energy (servers). Draw a circle representing total facility, then a smaller circle inside for IT - the ratio between them is PUE.
Picture alternating server rows: front-to-front = hot aisle, front-to-back = cold aisle. Hot air rises and is contained, cold air is directed to server intakes.
Draw energy entering data center as 100%, split into IT equipment (~50%) and overhead (cooling ~35%, power loss ~10%, other ~5%)

analogies.analogy: Just like a city needs power plants, water treatment, and transportation to function, a data center needs power systems, cooling, and networking infrastructure - all consuming energy.
One physical building (server) divided into multiple apartments (virtual machines), each with own resources but sharing common infrastructure.
PUE is like fuel efficiency: your car uses fuel for moving (IT work) but also for AC, lights, and engine losses. Lower PUE = more efficient like miles per gallon.
